rows.description: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.notes: Top-centre page title. Several letters are blurred/ornamental (esp. middle words). I transcribed literally as it appears; uncertain on proper names (Inguls/Guer). Title has no monetary amount.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.

rows.description: co Fine Gul: John
rows.notes: First entry under the title. Pounds '4' and shillings '15' align clearly between the ruling lines; pence reads '6' (clear). Vertical alignment of numerals is consistent with totals below.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Fine paid to John (ledger records as Gul: John).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine paid to John (ledger records as Gul: John), amounting to £4 15s 6d.

rows.description: co Fine Tho: Penn
rows.notes: Second row. Pounds column appears blank/zero; shillings read '10' clearly; pence appears to be '00' (no fraction). Slight ink fade on pounds column but alignment is clear.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Fine paid to Thomas Penn.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine paid to Thomas Penn, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.description: pro Rec. de Recd. de yardington
rows.notes: Third entry. Description pale and abbreviated; I read 'pro Rec. de Recd. de yardington' by context. Amount columns show '1' in pounds, '14' shillings, and '9' pence; digits slightly cramped but within column bounds.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Payment relating to Yardington (receipt/receipt-related charge).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment relating to Yardington (receipt/receipt-related charge), amounting to £1 14s 9d.

rows.description: pro fine Gul: Hopkins
rows.notes: Entry label legible. Amount looks like '0' pounds, '7' shillings, '00' pence. Pence column faint but consistent with zero.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Fine paid to Hopkins.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine paid to Hopkins, amounting to £0 7s 0d.

rows.description: pro fine Turney
rows.notes: Name reads 'Turney' (initial letter could be T or J but context suggests Turney). Pounds '5' and shillings '19' clear; pence '6' visible though ink slightly heavier here.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Fine paid to Turney.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine paid to Turney, amounting to £5 19s 6d.

rows.description: pro Rec: De yardington
rows.notes: Similar wording to earlier yardington line. Shillings read '19' clearly. Pence reads like '1' with a small trailing loop interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). The fraction loop is faint; moderate uncertainty.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Payment relating to Yardington (receipt/receipt-related charge).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment relating to Yardington (receipt/receipt-related charge), amounting to £0 19s 1½d.

rows.description: pro fine Jos: Harrington
rows.notes: Name reads as 'Jos: Harrington'. Amount columns show '0' pounds, '13' shillings, '1' pence with a small loop indicating a halfpenny; loop is moderately clear.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Fine paid to Joseph Harrington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine paid to Joseph Harrington, amounting to £0 13s 1½d.

rows.description: pro fine Jas: Jennings Arm:
rows.notes: Longer name 'Jas: Jennings Arm.' Pounds column appears to be '23' (two digits close together); shillings '13'. Pence reads '11' with a halfpenny loop. The two-digit pounds are slightly crowded and the pence fraction faint, hence moderate-low confidence.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.
rows.english_description: Fine paid to James Jennings (armiger).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fine paid to James Jennings, amounting to £23 13s 11½d.

rows.notes: Total line for the fines block. Numerals align neatly under columns and fraction is a clear three-quarters mark (looks like '3q' or three-quarters). Very clear alignment with ruling reference.
rows.section_header: Fines solut Scholari de Inguls Guer. & Sons.

rows.description: Bursaris pro dec: domus
rows.notes: Section label introducing bursary payments. No monetary amount on this line; centred/offset, readable.

rows.description: mrs Webber pro dec: Dom: 2d Feb. Nat. 1737
rows.notes: Name and date readable. Amount columns show '4' pounds, '00' shillings, '1' pence with a small halfpenny loop. Ink in pence is faint; moderate uncertainty on the fractional loop.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mrs Webber relating to the house account, dated 2 February 1737.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mrs Webber relating to the house account, dated 2 February 1737, amounting to £4 0s 1½d.

rows.description: Corn prices ad Egl. Pur. Anni & Mie.
rows.notes: The phrase 'Corn prices ad Egl. Pur. Anni & Mie.' is legible. Amount looks like '00' pounds, '03' shillings, '00' pence. Columns align well.
rows.english_description: Payment for corn (grain) prices / provision costs.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for corn (grain) prices / provisions, amounting to £0 3s 0d.

rows.description: Total (small line) 04 03 01 1/2
rows.notes: Small subtotal under the bursaries/corn prices. Numerals align beneath earlier amounts; pence shows a halfpenny. Slight ink fade on pence fraction.

rows.description: Soluta Varia.
rows.notes: Clear centered section heading 'Soluta Varia.' marking start of many dated disbursements. Very legible.

rows.description: 1737 Nov: 3 allowed mr Boyse one years Landtax
rows.notes: Date and description clear. Amount reads '00' pounds, '08' shillings, '11' pence (no fraction). Numerals well aligned.
rows.english_description: One year's land tax paid to Mr Boyse.
rows.english_description_with_amount: One year's land tax paid to Mr Boyse, amounting to £0 8s 11d.

rows.description: Nov: 18 pd the Painters Bill
rows.notes: Description 'pd the Painters Bill' clear. Amount columns clearly show '3' pounds, '18' shillings, '3' pence. Good ink and alignment.
rows.english_description: Painter's bill paid.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Painter's bill paid, amounting to £3 18s 3d.

rows.description: Nov: 23 to Mr Holt for the use of the Library
rows.notes: Entry clearly reads 'to Mr Holt for the use of the Library'. Amount '10' pounds and '10' shillings align well; pence '00'. High confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Holt for use of the library.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Holt for use of the library, amounting to £10 10s 0d.

rows.description: forgot to be inserted in ye last year's acct & pd to Mrs
rows.notes: Long marginal note; the latter part of the description is cut off by ink but the gist is clear. Amount columns read '0' pounds '6' shillings '00' pence; faint but legible.
rows.english_description: Payment omitted from the previous year's accounts and paid to a Mrs (name not recorded).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment omitted from the previous year's accounts and paid to a Mrs (name not recorded), amounting to £0 6s 0d.

rows.description: pd Mr Brooks his fee for stamps engraving & lettering as publick Notary & citation for Election of a new Rectr
rows.notes: Long descriptive line; words 'Brooks', 'stamps', 'engraving' legible. Amount '0' pounds, '15' shillings, '6' pence read clearly though description is dense.
rows.english_description: Fee paid to Mr Brooks for notary services, engraving and citation for the election of a new rector.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Fee paid to Mr Brooks for notary services, engraving and citation for the election of a new rector, amounting to £0 15s 6d.

rows.description: Dec: 14 pd Mr Baylis from June 5 1736
rows.notes: Date and name 'Baylis' clear. Amount '3.13.6' stands under columns distinctly.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Baylis covering sums due from 5 June 1736.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Baylis covering sums due from 5 June 1736, amounting to £3 13s 6d.

rows.description: Dec: 19 pd Mr Combes for Paper &c
rows.notes: Name looks like 'Combes' (the 'b' is slightly ambiguous). Amount reads '2' pounds, '00' shillings, '4' pence. Alignment clear; minor name uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Combes for paper and sundry stationery items.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Combes for paper and sundry stationery items, amounting to £2 0s 4d.

rows.description: Dec: 24 pd Simon Stubbs his Bill
rows.notes: Name 'Simon Stubbs' and 'his Bill' legible. Amount '3.17.6' is clearly written and aligned with column lines.
rows.english_description: Payment of Simon Stubbs's bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Simon Stubbs's bill, amounting to £3 17s 6d.

rows.description: Jan: 15 pd Mr Greenslade for Paper
rows.notes: Surname reads Greenslade (could be Greenslder/Greenslade); amount '0.8.5' visible. Slight ambiguity in letters of name, amount alignment clear.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Greenslade for paper.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Greenslade for paper, amounting to £0 8s 5d.

rows.description: Feb: 1 pd Mr Gaynier for his Visit at Mice
rows.notes: Handwriting for this line is cramped; name looks like 'Gaynier' and 'Visit at Mice' is uncertain wording. Amount appears to be '0.1.0' but digits are small and smudged; low confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Gaynier for his visit at Mice (visit-related fee).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Gaynier for his visit at Mice, amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.description: Feb: 3 pd John Bignell for painting letters in ye Library
rows.notes: Name 'John Bignell' and purpose 'painting letters in ye Library' readable. Amount '0.7.5' aligns in columns but pence is somewhat faded.
rows.english_description: Payment to John Bignell for painting letters in the library.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to John Bignell for painting letters in the library, amounting to £0 7s 5d.

rows.description: Feb: 11 pd Charncas? for Morton's
rows.notes: The payee name is very unclear (read as 'Charncas?' — uncertain). Amount column suggests '0.6.0' but overall row is faint and ambiguous. Low confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to an unclear-named payee for Morton's account (transcription uncertain).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to an unclear-named payee for Morton's account (transcription uncertain), amounting to £0 6s 0d.

rows.description: Feb: 21 pd Mr Raine's Bill
rows.notes: Name 'Raine' readable; pounds appear to be '16' (two digits close), shillings '14' legible. Ink heavier here but two-digit pounds produce moderate uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Payment of Mr Raine's bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Mr Raine's bill, amounting to £16 14s 0d.

rows.description: Mar: 23 pd Lewis subscription to Mr Robinson's Hesiod
rows.notes: The phrase 'subscri[p]tion to Mr Robinson's Hesiod' legible. Amount columns read '0.10.6' clearly.
rows.english_description: Payment for Lewis's subscription to Mr Robinson's edition of Hesiod.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for Lewis's subscription to Mr Robinson's edition of Hesiod, amounting to £0 10s 6d.

rows.description: Mar: 3 allowed mr Sadler for ye Progress
rows.notes: Name 'Sadler' and 'for ye Progress' readable. Amount appears '0.6.0' and aligns with columns; some smudging in description only.
rows.english_description: Allowance paid to Mr Sadler for the Progress (inspection tour expenses).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowance paid to Mr Sadler for the Progress (inspection tour expenses), amounting to £0 6s 0d.

rows.description: Mar: 6 pd Pavier for lighting ye College Lamps
rows.notes: Clear entry 'Pavier for lighting ye College Lamps'. Amount '0.10.0' clearly written.
rows.english_description: Payment to Pavier for lighting the college lamps.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Pavier for lighting the college lamps, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.description: Mar: 17 pd Mr Wentworth for a Copper plate of Coll. Arms for ???
rows.notes: Purpose indicates 'a Copper plate of Coll. Arms' legible; final word(s) faded. Amount '0.10.0' visible. Moderate uncertainty due to trailing words.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Wentworth for a copper plate of the college arms.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Wentworth for a copper plate of the college arms, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.description: 1738 Apr: 3 pd for mending ye Lamp
rows.notes: Date '1738 Apr: 3' and phrase 'mending ye lamp' clear. Amount reads '0.3.6' within columns; clear alignment and digits.
rows.english_description: Payment for mending a lamp.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for mending a lamp, amounting to £0 3s 6d.

rows.description: Apr: 3 pd Mr Gaynier 1/2 years salary at Decay
rows.notes: Entry wording partly ambiguous ('at Decay' uncertain); name 'Gaynier' reappears. Amount appears very small '0.1.0' but ink is faint; low confidence.
rows.english_description: Half-year's salary paid to Mr Gaynier (half-year payment).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year's salary paid to Mr Gaynier, amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.description: Apr: 3 pd Green's Bill for Candles
rows.notes: This row is problematic: the shillings appear as '32' which violates 0–19 rule, indicating I have misread column boundaries. The original looks like '1' (pound) and '12/??' shillings; due to illegibility I set low confidence. I include the digits as seen but flag this as likely misaligned — reviewer should re-check column boundary here.
rows.english_description: Payment of Green's bill for candles supplied to the college.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Green's bill for candles, amounting to £0 32s 5d.

rows.description: pd Mr Wentworth re printing of 2000 of Coll. Arms for ye Coll
rows.notes: Long description referencing 'printing of 2000 of Coll. Arms'. Amount reads '1.10.0' but placement of the '1' and the following digits were slightly crowded; moderate confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Wentworth for printing 2,000 copies of the college arms for the college.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Wentworth for printing 2,000 college arms, amounting to £1 10s 0d.

rows.description: Apr: 9 pd Mr Gosset a years stipend due to Mr Andrew on account of his having been appointed Travelling Fellow
rows.notes: Detailed description legible. Amount columns show '13' pounds, '6' shillings, '8' pence; two-digit pounds and pence are somewhat faded but readable.
rows.english_description: Payment by Mr Gosset of a year's stipend due to Mr Andrew on account of his appointment as Travelling Fellow.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment by Mr Gosset of a year's stipend due to Mr Andrew for his appointment as Travelling Fellow, amounting to £13 6s 8d.

rows.description: Apr: 22 pd Mr Collier for fixing ye Dining room floor in ye Parrish ???
rows.notes: Payee looks like 'Collier' and purpose 'fixing ye Dining room floor' readable. Amount '5.3.2' not crisp; some letters of description obscured by ink blot; moderate-low confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Collier for repairing the dining-room floor in the parish.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Collier for fixing the dining-room floor in the parish, amounting to £5 3s 2d.

rows.description: Apr: 30 spent on ye farmers at Yarnton on treating ?? for [illegible]
rows.notes: Description heavily abbreviated and final words faded. The amount '0.11.0' is legible but the description is uncertain; low confidence.
rows.english_description: Expenditure to the farmers at Yarnton for treating (purpose illegible).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Expenditure to the farmers at Yarnton for treating (purpose illegible), amounting to £0 11s 0d.

rows.description: May 6 allowed to Mr Dagumbe for what he laid out for wine at a meeting of ye Society in ye Bursary
rows.notes: The payee name 'Dagumbe' is uncertain (letters unclear). Purpose mentions wine at a meeting; amount shows '0.7.0' but letters are faint; low confidence.
rows.english_description: Reimbursement to Mr Dagumbe for wine purchased for a society meeting held in the bursary.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Reimbursement to Mr Dagumbe for wine bought for a society meeting in the bursary, amounting to £0 7s 0d.

rows.description: May 20 pd Mr Green as our part for designing of Oxford almanack
rows.notes: Entry clearer: 'Mr Green' and 'designing of Oxford almanack' legible. Amount '0.3.0' shown clearly though small.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Green as the college's contribution for the design of the Oxford almanack.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Green as the college's share for designing the Oxford almanack, amounting to £0 3s 0d.

rows.description: May 20 pd Mordon in full
rows.notes: This extraction is likely in error: pence '15' is outside 0–11, indicating misalignment of columns or mis-copy. The descriptive name 'Mordon' (or Morton) is legible, but numeric columns here are unclear; I flag this for re-examination. Low confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment made in full to Mordon in settlement of his account.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment made in full to Mordon in settlement of his account, amounting to £0 8s 15d.

rows.description: June 12 pd Second subscription for Dr. Havers Book
rows.notes: Description 'Second subscription for Dr Haver's Book' mostly legible. Amount appears '0.8.11' though ink in shillings/pence is faint; moderate confidence.
rows.english_description: Second subscription payment toward Dr Haver's book.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Second subscription payment for Dr Havers's book, amounting to £0 8s 11d.

rows.description: July 11 pd Jaques his Account
rows.notes: Payee 'Jaques' is legible. Amount '0.8.11' visible but faint; some ambiguity due to a smudge in the pence column. Moderate-low confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to Jaques in settlement of his account.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Jaques on account, amounting to £0 8s 11d.

rows.notes: Final grand total at lower right. Pounds appear as '129', shillings '09', pence '03' with halfpenny indicated by small loop. Numerals distinct though the page corner causes slight shading.
